scholarships.name: Rhodes Scholarship
scholarships.award_amount: full funding for graduate study at the University of Oxford
scholarships.description: Fully funded postgraduate scholarship for study at the University of Oxford. The U.S. constituency is open to U.S. citizens, permanent residents, and DACA recipients aged roughly 18-23 (or under 27 with a late first degree) and requires institutional endorsement.

verification.notes: 2027 Rhodes open; U.S. districts; opens Jul 1 2026, closes Oct 7 2026 11:59pm ET; citizen/PR/DACA eligible. Checklist verified 2026-06-24 from Rhodes Trust pages; detailed U.S. component list is in linked PDF guidance.

application_requirements.label: Complete online Rhodes application for your constituency
application_requirements.details: U.S. applicants apply through the Office of the American Secretary online system at rhodesscholar.org.

application_requirements.label: Obtain institutional endorsement
application_requirements.details: U.S. Rhodes application requires institutional endorsement, typically from a president, dean, or designated fellowship officer.

application_requirements.label: Submit referee letters
application_requirements.details: Referee letters are required in addition to endorsement. Official site publishes separate U.S. Endorsement and Referee guidance documents.

application_requirements.label: Apply for Oxford postgraduate study
application_requirements.details: Rhodes Scholarship supports full-time postgraduate study at the University of Oxford.

scholarships.name: Marshall Scholarship
scholarships.award_amount: full funding for graduate study in the United Kingdom
scholarships.description: Funds up to 50 U.S. citizens per year for graduate study in any field at any UK university. Requires a first undergraduate degree (minimum 3.7 GPA) earned within roughly two years of applying; applicants who already hold a UK degree are ineligible.

verification.notes: Up to 50 U.S. citizens/yr for UK graduate study; 3.7 GPA; institutional endorsement; 2026 cycle closed Sep 16 2025 — 2027 opens late spring 2026.

scholarships.name: Gates Cambridge Scholarship
scholarships.sponsor: Gates Cambridge Trust, University of Cambridge
scholarships.award_amount: full-cost scholarship for postgraduate study at the University of Cambridge
scholarships.description: Full-cost postgraduate scholarship at the University of Cambridge for students from outside the UK (about 25 awards in the dedicated U.S. round each year), available in any subject. Selection emphasizes academic excellence, leadership, and commitment to improving others' lives.

verification.notes: Official timeline says applications open September 2026 for 2027/8 entry. U.S. citizens resident in the USA apply in October 2026 (date to be confirmed); other applicants use course funding deadlines in December 2026 or early January 2027. Checklist verified 2026-06-24 from how-to-apply page.

application_requirements.label: Submit Cambridge graduate admission and funding application
application_requirements.details: Apply via the University of Cambridge Graduate Application Portal. Complete admission to a course/college place and the Gates Cambridge funding subsection.

application_requirements.label: Four Gates Cambridge funding questions
application_requirements.details: Four short statements mapped to selection criteria: academic excellence; why this Cambridge course; commitment to improving others' lives; leadership capacity.

application_requirements.label: Two academic references for admission
application_requirements.details: Standard two academic references required for Cambridge course admission as part of the combined application.

application_requirements.label: Gates Cambridge reference
application_requirements.details: Additional reference assessing suitability against Gates criteria. Referee also ranks applicant within a defined group.

scholarships.name: Schwarzman Scholars
scholarships.award_amount: full scholarship for a one-year master's at Tsinghua University
scholarships.description: Fully funded one-year Master's in Global Affairs at Tsinghua University in Beijing, open to applicants of all majors from any country (about 45% of scholars are from the U.S.). Applicants must be 18-28 with a completed undergraduate degree and strong English proficiency.

verification.notes: One-year Master's in Global Affairs at Tsinghua; ages 18-28; U.S./global app open Apr 8–Sep 9 2026 3pm EDT for 2027-28 class.

scholarships.name: Churchill Scholarship
scholarships.sponsor: Winston Churchill Foundation of the United States
scholarships.award_amount: full funding for one year of graduate study at the University of Cambridge
scholarships.description: Funds one year of master's study in STEM fields (science, mathematics, engineering, computer science) at Churchill College, University of Cambridge, for U.S. citizens. Applicants must be nominated by one of the participating U.S. institutions.

verification.notes: Official page says U.S. citizens at participating institutions need institutional nomination; next nomination deadline is Nov 2 2026 at 5pm ET. Selection criteria state there is no minimum GPA, though most applicants have a very high average.

scholarships.name: Knight-Hennessy Scholars
scholarships.award_amount: full funding for a Stanford graduate degree
scholarships.description: Fully funded scholarship supporting any full-time Stanford graduate degree (e.g., MA, MS, MBA, JD, MD, PhD); open to applicants of all fields from any country. Requires a bachelor's degree earned in 2020 or later and separate admission to a Stanford graduate program.

verification.notes: 2027 cohort open; any-country applicants; separate Stanford grad app due by Dec 1 2026; KHS deadline Oct 6 2026 1pm PT. Application checklist verified 2026-06-24 from Knight-Hennessy application pages.

application_requirements.label: Submit Knight-Hennessy Scholars online application
application_requirements.details: Separate KHS application required in English via the online portal. Opens June 1, 2026; deadline October 6, 2026, 1:00 PM Pacific.

application_requirements.label: Apply concurrently to a Stanford graduate program
application_requirements.details: Must apply to, be accepted by, and enroll in a full-time Stanford graduate degree program starting the same year, unless a listed exception applies.

application_requirements.label: Resume, transcript, and test scores
application_requirements.details: KHS requires resume, transcript, and test scores if required by your Stanford graduate program(s).

application_requirements.label: Two recommendation letters
application_requirements.details: Two recommenders who can address KHS criteria with specific examples. Due by the KHS deadline; you may submit before letters are received.

application_requirements.label: Short answers and essay
application_requirements.details: Three short answers (250/250/150 word limits) plus one 550-word essay.

scholarships.name: Forty Acres Scholars Program
scholarships.sponsor: Texas Exes (The University of Texas at Austin)
scholarships.award_amount: full cost of attendance (tuition, living stipend, books, and enrichment)

eligible_schools.name: The University of Texas at Austin

scholarships.description: Premier full-ride merit scholarship for incoming UT Austin freshmen, administered by the Texas Exes. Apply for UT Austin admission via the Common App or ApplyTexas by December 1 to be considered automatically; there is no separate Forty Acres application. Transfer and international students are not eligible for this program.

application_requirements.label: Submit a UT Austin first-year application
application_requirements.details: Apply through the Common App or ApplyTexas. Priority deadline October 15; final deadline December 1. There is no separate Forty Acres application; applying for UT Austin admission enters you as a candidate, and only selected students are contacted to continue.

application_requirements.label: Business applicants: also apply to the Canfield Business Honors Program
application_requirements.details: If you are applying to McCombs (business), the program advises also applying to the Canfield Business Honors Program.

application_requirements.label: If selected, complete the Forty Acres interview and requested materials
application_requirements.details: Only students selected to move forward are contacted directly by the program to continue the selection process, which includes interviews.

verification.notes: Texas Exes apply page: Dec 1 final deadline, U.S. citizen or permanent resident, automatic consideration via UT admission.

scholarships.name: Opportunity Award
scholarships.sponsor: Texas A&M University Scholarships & Financial Aid
scholarships.description: Freshman scholarship for entering Texas A&M students with outstanding high school records, leadership, and activities; some awards also consider financial need. Apply via Common App or ApplyTexas for admission and complete the Self-Reported Academic Record (STAR) by December 1. Texas A&M does not stack Opportunity Awards.

application_requirements.label: Submit a Texas A&M first-year application
application_requirements.details: Apply for admission through the Common App or ApplyTexas. The freshman scholarship application opens August 1 and closes December 1.

application_requirements.label: Complete the Self-Reported Academic Record (STAR)
application_requirements.details: Complete STAR by December 1; the admission application together with STAR is what places you in freshman scholarship consideration.

application_requirements.label: Submit a financial aid application (FAFSA or TASFA) for need-based awards
application_requirements.details: Some Opportunity Awards consider financial need; submit a financial aid application to be considered for the need-based awards.

verification.notes: Aggie One Stop Opportunity Award page: Aug 1-Dec 1 window, STAR required, financial aid application for need-based awards, $100-$15,000 range, no stacking.

scholarships.name: Gold Scholars Program
scholarships.sponsor: Georgia Institute of Technology Office of Scholar Programs
scholarships.award_amount: Georgia residents: tuition, fees, and books (~$12,500/year); out-of-state: non-resident tuition waiver (~$21,000/year)
scholarships.description: Four-year merit scholarship and scholar community for top incoming Georgia Tech freshmen (~10-20 per year), selected on scholarship, leadership, progress, and service. No separate application; apply for first-year admission by Early Action (Georgia residents: October 15; non-Georgia students: November 2 for fall 2027 entry).

application_requirements.label: Submit a Georgia Tech Early Action application
application_requirements.details: Apply by October 15 if you are a Georgia resident or November 2 if you are not. There is no separate Gold Scholars application and no additional materials are accepted; selection is made from your admission application.

application_requirements.label: Write a strong essay and fully document your activities in that application
application_requirements.details: Because no extra materials are accepted, the personal essay and activities sections of your Georgia Tech admission application are the basis for Gold Scholars selection.

verification.notes: Gold Scholars benefits page plus GT admission deadlines: scholarship consideration requires Early Action admission application.

scholarships.name: Academic Excellence Scholarship (AES)
scholarships.sponsor: The University of Texas at Dallas
scholarships.award_amount: varies by cohort (recipients qualify for in-state tuition and fees)

eligible_schools.name: The University of Texas at Dallas

scholarships.description: Merit scholarship for first-time-in-college freshmen admitted to UT Dallas for the fall term immediately after high school graduation. The admission application serves as the AES application; holistic review may consider optional essay, resume, and recommendations. AES recipients qualify for in-state tuition regardless of residency.

application_requirements.label: Submit a UT Dallas first-year application
application_requirements.details: Apply through the Common App or ApplyTexas by December 1. The admission application is the AES application; there is no separate AES form.

application_requirements.label: Submit official high school transcripts
application_requirements.details: Official high school transcripts are required as part of the application materials reviewed for AES.

application_requirements.label: Add the encouraged supplemental materials
application_requirements.details: Encouraged but not required: a resume, one essay on any topic, and up to three letters of recommendation strengthen your holistic review.

application_requirements.label: Complete the FAFSA (encouraged)
application_requirements.details: UT Dallas encourages completing the FAFSA, though it is not required for AES consideration.

application_requirements.label: If offered an AES award, accept it by June 1
application_requirements.details: The deadline to accept an AES award offer is June 1.

verification.notes: UT Dallas AES prospective page: Dec 1 deadline, fall freshman only, no separate AES application.

scholarships.name: CMU Pathway Program
scholarships.award_amount: tuition-free for families under $75k/year; meets 100% demonstrated need for eligible domestic undergraduates
scholarships.description: Need-based institutional aid for admitted Carnegie Mellon undergraduates. CMU does not offer general merit scholarships; admitted domestic students may receive grants through the Pathway Program (including tuition-free attendance for families earning under $75,000) after submitting the FAFSA and CSS Profile. Aid deadlines follow CMU financial aid timelines, not a single merit-scholarship date.

application_requirements.label: Submit the FAFSA for CMU financial aid
application_requirements.details: CMU requires both the FAFSA and CSS Profile for need-based aid. Forms open October 1; CMU's preferred deadlines are about November 15 (Early Decision) and February 15 (Regular Decision).

application_requirements.label: Submit the CSS Profile for CMU financial aid
application_requirements.details: Required alongside the FAFSA. Same preferred deadlines: about November 15 (Early Decision) and February 15 (Regular Decision).

application_requirements.label: Provide signed tax documents if CMU requests them
application_requirements.details: CMU may request signed parent and student tax returns, W-2s, or other supporting documents to verify your aid application.

verification.notes: CMU SFS FAQ confirms no general merit scholarships; costs-aid page confirms Pathway thresholds and full-need commitment.
